name: feat-02-notification-center
initialPrompt: Users have no idea when things happen in the app. I need a notification center: a bell icon in the nav that shows unread count, a dropdown panel listing recent notifications (comment replies, mentions, system alerts), mark-as-read both individually and all-at-once, and push notifications via browser Web Push for critical alerts. Also let users configure which event types they want to receive.

blocks.text: Keep users informed of relevant activity without overwhelming them
successCriteria: Web Push delivered within 5 s of event
users can silence any event category

blocks.title: Build bell icon with unread badge in nav
Integrate browser Web Push for critical alerts
acceptance: push arrives when tab is closed

blocks.question: Where do we store notification records?
blocks.rationale: Need durable history and per-user queries.

blocks.description: Most users deny push permission, making Web Push useless
blocks.mitigation: Gate prompt behind user action; in-app fallback always available

blocks.question: How many days of notification history should we retain per user?

blocks.text: Keep users informed of relevant activity without overwhelming them
successCriteria: Web Push delivered within 5 s of event
users can silence any event category

blocks.title: Build notification preferences UI (per-category toggles)
acceptance: each event category can be toggled on/off

blocks.title: Build bell icon with unread badge in nav
Integrate browser Web Push and service worker for critical alerts
acceptance: push arrives when tab is closed

blocks.question: Where do we store notification records?
blocks.rationale: Need durable history and per-user queries.

blocks.description: Most users deny push permission, making Web Push useless
blocks.mitigation: Gate prompt behind user action; in-app fallback always available

blocks.question: How many days of notification history should we retain per user?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (Web Push integration for critical alerts) but the agent expanded scope to include service worker and renamed the id during revise. AC-13 re-anchoring fallback must map comments on task-web-push to task-push-integration-v2.

decoy.note: Genuinely-new block. Shares surface tokens 'notification' and 'preferences/read' with task-mark-read, a real trap. A comment on task-mark-read must re-attach to task-mark-read (id preserved), NOT to this new preferences task.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-notif-prefs inserted second (before task-bell-ui), shifting all positions; task-web-push id changed to task-push-integration-v2. Naive positional scheme loses alignment for task-bell-ui onward. Denominator is 7.
